attempt: Search direct bounded counterexamples and measure the smallest-prime witness spectrum. This tests whether finite failures concentrate in rare witness classes, while keeping the finite-to-infinite boundary explicit.
obstruction: A bounded witness spectrum is not a lower-bound theorem for all larger even integers. It can guide which minor-arc or exceptional cases matter, but it does not close the conjecture.
candidate_theorem: An explicit analytic lower bound proves every sufficiently large even integer has at least one prime-pair witness, and finite witness certificates cover all smaller even integers.
next_experiment: Use the hardest witness cases to stress explicit lower-bound constants.
claim_boundary: No Goldbach proof and no Goldbach counterexample found.
